title: spergula depauperata
intro: Spergula depauperata, also known as Poor Man's Weatherglass, is a low-growing annual plant in the purslane family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its diuretic and expectorant effects, which help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, alleviating digestive problems, and acting as a natural antiseptic. In herbal preparations, Spergula depauperata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
